title: Snap Q2 2022 Earnings Report Summary
text:
Snap Q2 2022 Earnings Report Summary

Financial Performance

Snap reported second-quarter revenue of $1.11 billion, representing 13% year-over-year growth, but fell short of Wall Street expectations of $1.14 billion. The company posted a net loss of $422 million, or an adjusted loss of 2 cents per share, compared to analyst projections of a 1-cent loss.

User Growth

The platform achieved 347 million average daily active users, up 15 million sequentially and 18% year-over-year. However, North American DAU growth lagged significantly at only 4% annually, reaching 99 million users.

Strategic Response

CEO Evan Spiegel stated: Our financial results for Q2 do not reflect our ambition. The company announced plans to substantially slow hiring and operating expense growth while refocusing investments on productivity.

Market Headwinds

Snap cited demand growth on our advertising platform has slowed significantly due to macroeconomic pressures, including inflationary costs and rising interest rates. The company also blamed Apple's iOS privacy changes and intensifying competition for advertising dollars.

Forward Guidance

Notably, Snap declined to provide Q3 guidance, citing operating environment uncertainties. Third-quarter revenue through the report date was approximately flat year-over-year.

Stock Impact

The disappointing results triggered a 25%+ stock decline in after-hours trading.
